portal.mapOnlyDelivery: Live map applies to delivery orders only.
portal.configureCustomerUuid: Link this portal to a customer: set REACT_APP_PORTAL_CUSTOMER_ID in .env to the customer’s UUID from Supabase (Table Editor → customers → id), then restart the app. The demo value "1" is not a valid UUID.
portal.selectCustomerHint: Prototype: pick any customer to preview their orders, tracking, and invoices.
portal.selectCustomerFirst: Choose a customer above to load orders and invoices.

quality.qcTechSubtitle: Create tests for orders, run them, then approve to assign QC and mark orders ready for dispatch.
quality.fillRequiredFields: Please fill in all required fields (Lot Number and Product)

quality.invalidOrderSelected: Invalid order selected. Please select an order from the dropdown.

quality.testRejectedMessage: Test REJECTED. Lot cannot be dispatched.
quality.testCreatedSuccess: Test created successfully! You can now run the test.

quality.allOrdersQcOkMessage: All orders are either pending production or have passed QC

pdf.footer2: Certificate generated electronically and is valid without signature.

pickup.title: Pickup release
pickup.subtitle: Record truck weights and driver ID when the customer collects a pickup order (after QC / ready).
pickup.invalidWeights: Enter valid empty and loaded weights (loaded must be ≥ empty).
pickup.noEligible: No pickup orders waiting for release.
pickup.documentsHint: Upload DNI / ID, license, or other files (PDF or images). Stored in the documents bucket.

billing.title: Billing & Payment
billing.enterValidPaymentAmount: Please enter a valid payment amount
billing.cannotInvoiceNotDelivered: Cannot generate invoice: Order must be delivered first with signature captured.
billing.cannotInvoiceNoCert: Cannot generate invoice: Order must have a QC certificate. This is required for invoicing.
billing.cannotInvoicePickupNoRelease: Cannot generate invoice: Pickup orders must have a recorded pickup release (weights + driver ID) before invoicing.
billing.uploadFailed: Upload failed. Check the Storage bucket and your connection.
billing.couldNotOpenPdf: Could not open PDF. Try uploading again or check storage policies.
billing.confirmRemovePdf: Remove the uploaded invoice PDF from storage?
billing.pdfUploadSavesAutomatically: Choosing a file uploads it immediately — no separate Save step.
billing.pdfSavedToInvoice: PDF saved and linked to this invoice.
billing.invoiceDialogFooterHint: Use Close when you are done. Download opens the official PDF in a new tab.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.trackSubtitle: Track production and inventory levels. Use recommendations to maintain appropriate stock at each location (quarry, near-well).

production.title: Production
production.produceToInventorySubtitle: Add stock directly. No customer order required.
production.produceSuccess: Lot created. Go to Quality, run the test, and approve it. Inventory increases only after QC approval.
production.pendingQCLotsHint: Produce to inventory – run QC on Quality page

msa.fillRequiredFields: Please fill in all required fields

approvals.title: Approvals
approvals.subtitle: Two-level redirect approval: Level 1 (Middle Management) first, then Level 2 (Operations). New requests appear in Level 1.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.subtitle: Tasks assigned to your role. Open a task to view details.
tasks.tasksWillAppear: Tasks will appear here when there is work assigned to your role.

dispatcher.title: Dispatcher
dispatcher.subtitle: View orders, get recommendations, and assign warehouse or truck.
dispatcher.pickupNoRecommendations: This order is customer pickup. Truck and warehouse assignments do not apply — use Pickup release when the load leaves the yard.

approvals.subtitle: Approve assignment and redirect requests from the dispatcher.

rules.title: Recommendation rules
rules.subtitle: Configure rules that influence dispatcher recommendations (e.g. prefer warehouse, JIT-first).
rules.name: Name

guide.title: Workflow guide
guide.noSteps: No guide steps are available for the current role.
guide.onboardingMessage: New here? Open the workflow guide to learn orders, QC, dispatch, and more.
guide.previewHint: Static demo layout — not your live data.
guide.continueGuideHint: Use the Next button at the bottom to continue this guide, or Open this page to try it live.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.body: High-level KPIs, charts, and recent activity. Your landing page depends on role (admins often see this first).
instructionSteps: Scan the KPI cards for revenue, orders, deliveries, and QC health.
Use the charts to spot trends (e.g. revenue, status mix).
Check Recent activity for the latest order and delivery events.
If My tasks appears, use it as a shortcut — or open Tasks from the left menu.
To demo another persona, click the role menu in the top bar (person icon) and pick a role.
dashboard.next: Typically you create or review Orders, then work flows through Dispatcher, QC, Logistics or Pickup release, and Billing. Follow the next topics in this guide with Next.

orders.title: Orders
orders.body: Create customer orders and watch status from pending through production, QC, dispatch, and completion.
instructionSteps: Open Orders from the left menu (or use Open this page below).
Click Create order (usually top-right) to start a new order.
Select the customer, order type (MSA vs standalone PO if offered), and fulfillment: delivery to site vs customer pickup.
Fill delivery or pickup details, then add lines: product, quantity (tons), and price if required.
Save the order. Use search or filters on the list to find it again; click a row to open details and status.
Watch for status changes as production, QC, and dispatch update the order elsewhere in the app.
orders.next: When the order needs resources, Dispatcher assigns warehouse and truck (delivery). For pickup orders, you skip truck assignment and later use Pickup release at the yard.

dispatcher.title: Dispatcher
dispatcher.body: Assign warehouses and trucks to delivery orders; pickup orders are handled in Pickup release instead.
instructionSteps: Open Dispatcher from the left menu.
Use the fulfillment filter if you want only Delivery or only Pickup rows — pickup rows remind you to use Pickup release, not truck assignment.
Select an order and review recommendations (warehouse, truck) when shown.
Use Assign (or equivalent actions) to commit warehouse and truck for deliveries.
If something must be redirected, use the app’s redirect flow — approvals may appear under Approvals for managers.
After assignment, drivers and Logistics see the active run; pickup orders move forward via Pickup release when the load leaves.
dispatcher.next: Managers may approve certain requests under Approvals. QC runs tests under Quality. Active deliveries are tracked under Logistics.

approvals.title: Approvals
approvals.body: Two-level approval for some dispatcher requests (e.g. redirects): Jefatura first, then Operations when required.
instructionSteps: Open Approvals from the left menu.
Check Level 1 (Jefatura) for items waiting for first approval.
Approve or reject with a reason if prompted; rejected items stop until revised.
Switch to Level 2 (Operations) for items that need second-level approval.
Confirm which order and trucks/warehouses are affected before approving.
When approved at the right level, the dispatcher’s change can take effect in the system.
approvals.next: Dispatch continues with assignments; drivers see updates in Logistics. Rules (separate screen) influence future recommendations.

rules.title: Recommendation rules
rules.body: Business rules that shape dispatcher recommendations (priority, warehouse preference, urgency, etc.).
instructionSteps: Open Rules from the left menu.
Review existing rules: name, conditions, priority (lower often runs first — check your product’s behavior).
Add or edit a rule: set fields (e.g. region, product, order size), operator, and value.
Toggle Active so only intended rules apply.
Save changes and verify recommendations on Dispatcher after updates.
rules.next: Dispatchers use updated logic on the next recommendations. Combine with Approvals when policy changes need sign-off.

quality.title: Quality control
quality.body: Run tests, record results, and attach or issue certificates so orders can leave the QC stage.
instructionSteps: Open Quality from the left menu.
Find orders or samples that need tests (Tasks also lists QC work for your role).
Open a row, enter test results (e.g. gradation, moisture) and pass/fail as your process requires.
Attach or generate certificates when the workflow demands it.
Save so the order status can progress toward Ready / dispatch.
Use Refresh if data looks stale after another team updates the order.
quality.next: Passed QC orders can be scheduled by Dispatcher (delivery) or prepared for pickup. Failed tests may block dispatch until resolved.

logistics.title: Logistics & traceability
logistics.body: Live delivery tracking: map, route, and status from yard to customer site.
instructionSteps: Open Logistics from the left menu.
Select a delivery in the list or on the map to see details.
Follow status steps (loading, in transit, delivering) and ETA where shown.
Drivers: use this view for assigned runs; others use it to monitor progress and exceptions.
Customers (portal) see tracking for their own deliveries — not for pickup-only flows the same way.
logistics.next: When the truck completes delivery, statuses roll forward to billing and completion. Pickup orders use Pickup release instead of this map-centric flow.

pickupRelease.title: Pickup release
pickupRelease.body: Close out customer pickup at the yard: weights, driver ID, and completion — not truck assignment.
instructionSteps: Open Pickup release from the left menu when the customer collects at the yard.
Find the order (often filtered to ready pickup orders).
Enter gross and tare (or net) weights as your process defines.
Record driver or gate ID if required.
Click Complete release (or equivalent) to finalize — this moves the order toward picked up / completed.
If Dispatcher said the order is pickup, do not assign a delivery truck; use this screen instead.
pickupRelease.next: Finance may invoice the order under Billing; the customer portal can show pickup completed and invoices when configured.

billing.title: Billing
billing.body: Invoices, amounts, and PDFs — upload so customers can download from the portal when enabled.
instructionSteps: Open Billing from the left menu.
Locate the invoice row for the order or customer.
Update status (e.g. sent, paid) per your process.
Upload the invoice PDF where the app provides upload — save and close so the file is stored.
Confirm the customer portal can see the document if you rely on self-service downloads.
billing.next: Accounting tasks may also appear under Tasks. Customers see paid/open invoices in the portal when linked.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.body: Stock by site and product; align loads with what Dispatcher and production need.
instructionSteps: Open Inventory from the left menu.
Review quantities by warehouse or site and product grade.
Watch for alerts or low-stock indicators if your build shows them.
Coordinate with Dispatcher before promising loads that need scarce stock.
Refresh after production or movements update balances.
inventory.next: Production feeds inventory; Dispatcher consumes it for assignments. Fleet ties trucks to those movements.

fleet.title: Fleet
fleet.body: Trucks and availability — Dispatcher assigns vehicles to delivery runs.
instructionSteps: Open Fleet from the left menu.
Review each truck’s status (available, assigned, in transit, etc.).
Keep vehicle records current so Dispatcher does not assign unavailable units.
When a truck returns, update status so the next delivery can use it.
Pickup-only orders do not need fleet assignment for the customer haul-away path.
fleet.next: Assigned trucks appear in Logistics for active runs. Maintenance or downtime should be reflected here first.

production.title: Production
production.body: Volumes and capacity by product versus demand from open orders.
instructionSteps: Open Production from the left menu.
Compare output or capacity to what Orders and Inventory require.
Identify bottlenecks or shortfalls before promising new tonnage.
Align with Inventory sites so stock numbers stay credible for Dispatcher.
production.next: Higher production supports Inventory; QC still gates release of material to dispatch.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.body: Role-specific work queue: QC, dispatch, approvals, pickups, invoices, and more.
instructionSteps: Open Tasks from the left menu (bell icon in the top bar may show counts too).
Scan categories: QC tests, orders to assign, deliveries, approvals, etc.
Click a task row to jump to the right page with context.
Use this when you are unsure where to work next — it reflects current backlog.
Refresh after completing work elsewhere so the list updates.
tasks.next: Each task type maps to screens in this guide (Quality, Dispatcher, Approvals, Pickup release, Billing, …).

msas.title: MSAs
msas.body: Master service agreements drive pricing and terms on eligible orders.
instructionSteps: Open MSAs from the left menu.
Review active agreements and linked customers.
When creating an order, choose MSA-backed pricing where applicable so terms auto-apply.
Update documents when legal or commercial terms change.
Keep customer records in Customers aligned with who is under which MSA.
msas.next: New Orders should pick up MSA pricing automatically when configured. Billing references the same commercial relationship.

customers.title: Customers
customers.body: Customer master data for orders, portal identity, and MSAs.
instructionSteps: Open Customers from the left menu.
Add or edit name, region, and identifiers your process requires.
Link portal access or UUIDs when integrating Supabase or external IDs.
Use these records when creating Orders so routing and pricing stay consistent.
Deactivate or archive obsolete accounts per policy instead of deleting blindly.
customers.next: Orders and the customer portal both depend on correct customer rows. MSAs attach to these records.

customerPortal.title: Customer portal
customerPortal.body: Read-only customer view: orders, timeline, map (delivery), pickup details, and invoices.
instructionSteps: Open Customer portal from the left menu as a customer role (or preview).
If your deployment uses a customer selector, pick the company you represent.
Review order timeline: placed, in progress, QC, dispatched or pickup, delivered or picked up.
For deliveries, open tracking/map when available.
Download invoice PDFs after Accounting uploads them in Billing.
Pickup orders show yard pickup details instead of a live truck map.
customerPortal.next: Internally, teams continue on Orders, Dispatcher, Logistics, Pickup release, and Billing — the portal mirrors status for the customer.

orderForm.pricingAutoApplied: Pricing will be auto-applied from MSA
orderForm.pricingManualEntry: Pricing and terms must be entered manually for each product
orderForm.noteCustomerHasMSA: Note: Customer has an active MSA, but you're creating a standalone PO
